behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is request handoff between classifier module and API client, not model judgment. A pure unit test of a helper could miss wiring bugs where taxonomy is dropped before transport. Real OS/network is not required because capturing an in-process stubbed client request reproduces the failure. Rejected System: behavior text is about request content inclusion, not real network behavior. Rejected Agentic: no dependence on model output quality/content.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier + real request-construction path + stub transport client; invoke classify() and assert outbound payload contains the full configured taxonomy labels list for every call.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is output-normalization logic: given an out-of-taxonomy label, map to "unknown". The failure can be caught at one local function/object boundary with no I/O. Rejected Integration: behavior text does not require cross-module state propagation beyond passing one returned label value. Rejected Agentic/System: real model/network not needed to trigger invalid-label input.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization/mapping logic directly with taxonomy + invalid provider label fixture and assert result is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Determinism of normalization for identical input is a pure local transformation property. No module interaction, infra, or model variability is needed. Rejected higher levels: behavior explicitly scopes to identical raw responses and normalization step only.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke normalization repeatedly with the same raw response fixture and taxonomy; assert identical final label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live API calls and consistency of returned labels, which depends on real model behavior/non-determinism. A stub would always pass and hide real drift/variance. Rejected Workflow: this is repeated single-step classification calls, not a multi-step agent pipeline with sequential model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run N real provider calls for identical document+taxonomy, record raw provider outputs and final labels, and assert label consistency when provider outputs are unchanged; flag variance attributable to model response changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at module boundary between token-budget enforcement and dispatch path (e.g., over-budget request still sent). Needs wiring of config + builder + dispatch decision, but not real network/model. Rejected System: behavior is pre-dispatch enforcement, so in-process client spy/fake is sufficient. Rejected Agentic: no dependence on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier with real token-budget config and a spy/stub API client; submit over-budget and near-budget documents; assert truncate/reject policy is applied and dispatch is blocked or transformed per policy before any client call.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Verification requires a real provider call and real provider-reported usage metadata for the actual model invocation path. Mocked usage can hide failures in true provider accounting/model-side tokenization. Rejected Integration: would rely on stubbed usage and miss real provider behavior. Rejected Workflow: single call measurement, not multi-step agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real classification calls for representative documents against the provider/model configuration, collect provider-reported token usage, and assert usage is <= configured per-call budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is retry orchestration across client/error handling/backoff logic. Can be reproduced with a stub client returning rate-limit signal then success, plus controlled clock/sleeper. Rejected System: real network/subprocess not required to validate retry semantics. Rejected Agentic: correctness does not depend on model output, only transport/error codes and retry behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier with retry policy, fake clock/sleeper, and stub API client scripted to return rate-limit then success; assert wait/backoff invoked and final caller result is success, not propagated rate-limit error.
